title: Hunt Lesson v1
description:
A per-program hunting lesson captured in the recursive learning loop
(rejected submission -> dead-end lesson -> next hunt avoids it). One JSON
object per line in playbooks/<program>.jsonl. The human-readable markdown
playbook (playbooks/<program>.md) is GENERATED from this ledger — never
hand-edited; always go through `lab-hunt-lesson`.

Per the recursive-learning design (SI / hunt playbook):
  - Categories: dead_end, viable_surface, design_intent, what_worked,
    what_failed, oos_trap. (`what_failed` covers approaches that wasted
    time; `dead_end` covers investigated-and-confirmed non-issues; `oos_trap`
    covers out-of-scope patterns that look like bugs but are OOS per the
    program policy.)
  - `superseded: false` by default; set true by a later lesson that
    replaces this one (e.g. a dead_end later disproven becomes what_worked).
  - Idempotency: same (program, claim) pair = no-op. The CLI dedupes by
    the claim string; capture never generates a duplicate ledger line.

lesson_id.description: Stable identifier for this lesson. Convention: 'hunt-lesson-<uuid>'.

program.description:
Program slug this lesson belongs to (e.g. 'gitlab', 'notion', '_general').
'_general' is a cross-program playbook for lessons that apply broadly.

category.description:
Lesson category — controls how the hunting workflow consumes it.
- dead_end: investigated-and-confirmed non-issue. Do NOT report.
- viable_surface: promising attack surface worth testing.
- design_intent: behavior that is by design (check before reporting).
- what_worked: a finding approach that was confirmed to work.
- what_failed: an approach that wasted time (too large, no impact, OOS).
- oos_trap: out-of-scope pattern that looks like a bug but is OOS.

claim.description:
The lesson text. One falsifiable, actionable sentence. This is the
deduplication key — same (program, claim) = no-op.
examples: NuGet symbol server is unauthenticated by design — the 3-key lookup IS the security boundary

evidence.description:
Reference that grounds the claim (file:line, MR reference, report ID,
commit hash). null when the lesson is fully abstract.

agent.description:
Agent name (e.g. 'opencode', 'claude', 'codex') or 'record-outcome'
(for the automatic feedback loop from rejected submissions).

model.description:
Model identifier (e.g. 'glm-5.2', 'claude-opus-4-6'). null when
the lesson was captured by a non-LLM path (e.g. record-outcome).

superseded.description:
True when a later lesson replaces this one. False by default. The
markdown renderer skips superseded lessons unless --include-superseded is
passed.

examples.claim: NuGet symbol server is unauthenticated by design — the 3-key lookup IS the security boundary
